mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding half a percentage point over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or rank IC exceeds 0.04.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal income combined with stable inflation indicates robust consumer purchasing power without monetary erosion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank IC or positive return frequency falls below 50% across all tested walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase reflects strengthening corporate output and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency drops below 52% during confirmed recovery phases or rank IC turns negative over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real GDP growth confirm expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Walk-forward rank IC remains below 0.01 over 2-year windows or positive return frequency falls below 50% during expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation spikes exceeding 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 65% of high-inflation months or rank IC exceeds 0.05 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm broad-based labor market strength and rising wage income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a positive rank IC or positive return frequency drops below 53% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive return frequency exceeds 58% during late-cycle housing downturns or rank IC remains positive over 3-year rolling windows.
